description_long: This object is a modern traffic light assembly consisting of a sturdy vertical pole mounted on a broad, square base for stability. At the top, a horizontal arm extends outward, supporting a horizontally oriented set of three circular signal lights—red, yellow, and green—housed in a rectangular black casing. Below the main arm, a secondary vertical signal light with three stacked lenses is attached to the pole, also displaying red, yellow, and green lights. The structure is primarily matte black, with the colored lenses providing visual contrast. The design is typical for urban intersections, ensuring visibility for both vehicles and pedestrians.
description: A black pole-mounted traffic light with both horizontal and vertical signal lights, featuring red, yellow, and green lenses. The assembly is robust and designed for urban street intersections.
description_view_0: The front view shows both the horizontal and vertical signal lights clearly, with all three colored lenses visible on each.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the thickness of the signal casings and the depth of the pole, with the lights mostly obscured.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the signal casings and the supporting arm, with no colored lenses visible.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again shows the profile of the pole and the signal casings, with the lights not directly visible.
